steps.title: Identify the time signal
steps.content: "Born in 1979" — a specific year in the past. This points to a single completed event at a definite past moment.

steps.title: Choose the verb tense
steps.content:
Rule: simple past tense marks a completed action at a specific past time. "In 1979" requires simple past.
  • Choice A — "was": simple past of "be" — correctly marks a completed event at a specific past date. ✓
  • Choice B — "has been": present perfect — implies a connection to the present, but birth is a single completed past event with no ongoing relevance to "being born". ✗
  • Choice C — "is": simple present — contradicts "in 1979", a past time marker. ✗
  • Choice D — "is being": present progressive — describes an ongoing action happening now; birth is an instantaneous past event. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
